domain:
In an automated facility, a fleet of robots is deployed, where each one is recognized by a unique identifier. These robots perform tasks based on their function, some are cleaning robots, while others serve as transport robots. In the facility, the robots are assigned to a set of tasks, each distinguished by a unique code. Throughout the operation, a robot may be assigned to perform various tasks. 

When a robot performs a task, the execution  is identified by a unique code and occurs at a specific starting time. Finally, each robot is assigned to a location within the facility, which can be either a corridor or a room.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Robot in the relationship with Task?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a task can exist without any associated robot.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a task must have at least one associated robot.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Task can exist without Robot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Task must have at least one Robot.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Task can exist without Robot.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Robot in the relationship with Task?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a task is associated with exactly one robot.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a task can be associated with multiple robots.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each task links to one robot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each task can link to multiple robots.
questions.selected: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each task links to one robot.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Task in the relationship with Robot?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a robot can exist without any associated task.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a robot must have at least one associated task.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Robot can exist without Task.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Robot must have at least one Task.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Robot can exist without Task.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Task in the relationship with Robot?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a robot is associated with exactly one task.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a robot can be associated with multiple tasks.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each robot links to one task.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each robot can link to multiple tasks.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each robot can link to multiple tasks.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Task and TaskExecution be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, TaskExecution would depend on Task for its existence.
2. Use association, TaskExecution could exist independently of Task, and can also be linked to multiple tasks.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class TaskExecution depends on Task.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class TaskExecution exists independently of Task.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class TaskExecution depends on Task.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Task in the relationship with TaskExecution?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a task execution can exist without any associated task.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a task execution must have at least one associated task.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, TaskExecution can exist without Task.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,TaskExecution must have at least one Task.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,TaskExecution must have at least one Task.

questions.question:
Should TaskExecution be modeled as an association class linking Robot and Task, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: TaskExecution exists only as part of the relationship between Robot and Task. A task execution cannot exist without the link between Robot and Task.
2. Regular class: TaskExecution is a class that has links to Robot and Task. In this case multiple task executions can exist for every Robot and Task.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, TaskExecution as association class linking Robot and Task.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, TaskExecution as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, TaskExecution as association class linking Robot and Task.
